content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Three Paths on a Tree". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/1294/problem/F
constraints: The graph is a tree, i.e., it is connected and has no cycles.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, the number of nodes in the tree. Then n-1 lines follow, each containing two integers u and v, the nodes connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: The output should be the maximum product of the lengths of three paths in the tree.

hidden.label: uniform/edge cases
hidden.expectedOutput: Error: The input graph is not a tree.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed
hidden.expectedOutput: Error: The input graph is empty.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a depth-first search (DFS) to find all possible paths in the tree, then calculate the product of the lengths of all possible combinations of three paths.
